fingerprint: Pure duplicate of WIN-007 using identical IGRF/globe-coordinate data — no independent dome derivation for 18° threshold or 120°E reference
globe_value: WMM2025 (spherical harmonic model, IGRF coefficients) places magnetic pole at 139.298°E in globe-native longitude coordinates
dome_value: W010 threshold: NP deviation > 18° from 120°E (i.e., > 138°E). Confirmed at 139.298°E = +19.3°
match_precision: Exact — dome consumes WMM2025/NP.xy output verbatim with zero transformation
why_it_matters: Three-layer globe dependency: (1) The 'North Magnetic Pole position' is defined as where Earth's magnetic field is vertical — a concept requiring a spherical surface. On a flat disc, 'vertical' has no privileged meaning at the pole. (2) The coordinate 139.298°E is longitude, defined on a sphere. The dome's monitor.py fetches NOAA NP.xy and checks lon_obs > 130°E — a globe-coordinate threshold on globe-model data. (3) The 120°E reference meridian and 18° threshold have no derivation from dome parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, etc.). No formula on any dome page or in any repository file starts from dome geometry and arrives at either 18° or 120°E.

code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py: W010 exists only as static confirmed_wins title entry ('NP deviation > 18 deg from 120 deg E'). monitor.py: NMP Longitude domain fetches NOAA NP.xy, checks lon_obs > 130 (for WIN-007), uses lon_pred = 139.3. No separate domain for WIN-036's 18° from 120°E threshold. pull_data.py: fetches NMP via fetch_nmp() (imported), logs month/lat/lon/delta — no deviation calculation from 120°E.
code_search.notes: WIN-036 has ZERO code presence beyond a static title in inject_ai_layer.py. No monitoring domain, no threshold computation, no deviation formula. The confirmation was manual comparison: 139.298 - 120 = 19.3 > 18. This is arithmetic performed on globe-model output, not a dome prediction.

logical_entailment.relationship: WIN-007 confirms pole at 139.3°E (NOAA NP.xy). WIN-036 claims pole > 18° from 120°E (i.e., > 138°E). If pole is at 139.3°E (WIN-007), then 139.3 - 120 = 19.3 > 18 (WIN-036). WIN-036 is arithmetically entailed by WIN-007 — same data point, lower threshold.
logical_entailment.independent_information_content: Zero. One NOAA measurement confirms both WINs simultaneously.

batch_registration.gap_months: >14 months between data availability and prediction registration

suggested_text: The 120°E reference meridian and 18° threshold have no derivation from dome geometry — no formula in any dome source file starts from disc radius, firmament height, or aetheric parameters and arrives at either number. The dome's own monitor.py does not track this WIN; it monitors WIN-007's pole position at lon > 130°E using the same NOAA NP.xy globe-model data. WIN-036 adds zero independent information beyond WIN-007: if the pole is at 139.3°E (WIN-007), deviation from 120°E is 19.3° by subtraction — exceeding 18° is arithmetic, not prediction.
